title: argemone grandiflora subsp. grandiflora
intro: Argemone grandiflora subsp. grandiflora, also known as the Mexican prickly poppy, is a plant species belonging to the Papaveraceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds, which contain sanguinarine and dihydrosanguinarine, compounds that have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. The seeds of the plant have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Argemone grandiflora subsp. grandiflora can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat different health conditions.
